# Gygax

> TTRPG systems analysis and design collaboration

## About

| field | value |
|---|---|
| slug | `gygax` |
| version | `2.0.0` |
| schema_version | `3` |
| author | gumibera |
| license | MIT |

TTRPG systems analyst — design, balance, and stress-test tabletop RPG mechanics with persistent game-state, scenario-based playtesting, and design experimentation

## Skills

- `attune` — Onboard and ingest a TTRPG system, rulebook, or game reference into Gygax
- `augury` — Analyze balance of TTRPG mechanics through quantitative numerical methods
- `cabal` — Scenario-based playtest simulation with 9 player archetypes and experience tracking
- `gygax-status` — Display current Gygax construct status and game state overview
- `homebrew` — Design and refine TTRPG mechanics with cross-system consistency checking
- `lore` — Surface TTRPG design heuristics, patterns, and anti-patterns
- `scry` — Design experimentation through persistent forked game-state with inline analysis

## Commands

- `/attune` — Ingest a TTRPG source and build structured game-state.
- `/augury` — Run numerical balance analysis grounded in game-state.
- `/cabal` — Stress-test game mechanics with simulated player archetypes.
- `/gygax` — Show current game-state status, recent analysis, and active tensions.
- `/homebrew` — Design or refine a TTRPG mechanic with cross-system consistency checking.
- `/lore` — Apply curated TTRPG design heuristics by tradition.
- `/scry` — Explore design changes through persistent forked game-state with inline analysis.
